product.name: Nesta
product.dek: Nesta is a private messenger for one household — no public feed, no strangers, no ads. Grown-ups hold the keys; children join with a parent-issued QR and never need an email or a password.
product.note: Every feature below shows exactly where it lives in the app.

parts.title: From a blank phone to a family, in minutes
parts.dek: The setup path is deliberately short: one adult signs in and creates the household; everyone else joins through a grant — a QR, a link, or a short code.

features.title: Install or open anywhere
features.summary: Play Store, App Store/TestFlight, or the installable web app at nesta.tinykite.co. Nothing asks for an account until someone chooses to have one.

features.title: Adult sign-in
features.summary: Email & password, Google, Apple, or passkeys in the native apps; the web app hands off to the central account hub and returns signed in. Forgot-password works from the screen or the emailed link.

features.title: Create a household
features.summary: Name the family and yourself — you're the first admin and this device is active immediately. No code needed for the founder.

features.title: Add a child — first name only
features.summary: Type their first name; Nesta provisions a hidden account and hands you the complete pass-along: a QR code, a shareable link, and a typed fallback code. No password is shown because none is needed.

features.title: Child joins: confirm and you're in
features.summary: Scan the QR, tap the link, or type the code — the grant already knows who it's for, so the child sees “Setting up Rohan's device?”, taps Yes, that's me, and lands in the family chat. Zero typing, zero credentials.

features.title: Links & QR codes open the app
features.summary: The join link is a verified app link: with Nesta installed it opens the app directly (Android App Links, iOS Universal Links); without it, the web app takes over. The join screen also scans QR codes itself.

features.title: Invite another adult
features.summary: A one-time invite code for another grown-up, with their authority — member or co-admin — fixed at issue time.

features.title: Adult joins with an invite
features.summary: Enter the code, sign in or create an account, pick a chat name — the device activates automatically and lands in chat.
features.entry: Welcome → I was invited by my family

parts.title: The family's day-to-day
parts.dek: A familiar messenger, scoped to one household.

features.title: Chat home
features.summary: All conversations with previews, timestamps, and unread badges. The household group chat exists from day one.

features.title: Direct chats
features.summary: Pick any family member to open a one-to-one conversation.

features.title: Messages with real delivery ticks
features.summary: Pending, sent, delivered, read — plus date separators, an offline banner, and an outbox that retries failed sends or lets you discard them.

features.title: Photos, videos & files
features.summary: Attach from the gallery, shoot with the camera, or send a document — rendered as media bubbles with download handling.

features.title: Voice notes
features.summary: Hold the mic, watch the live level meter, slide to cancel; playback with waveform.

features.title: Notifications that respect privacy
features.summary: A push says WHO wrote — never what. The relay stays content-blind; tapping the alert deep-links into that conversation. iOS APNs and Android FCM, registered the moment a device is in.

parts.title: Warm, expressive, familiar
parts.dek: The little touches that make a family chat feel like family — react, reply, and add a bit of personality, all in the messenger you already know how to use.

features.title: Emoji that look the same everywhere
features.summary: An emoji button opens a searchable picker with skin tones and a recents tab. Every emoji shows as the same friendly glyph on iPhone, Android, and the web — so what you send is exactly what everyone sees — and a message of just one to three emoji arrives large and gently animated.

features.title: React to a message
features.summary: Long-press any message to react with an emoji; reactions gather into little animated chips with a count. One per person — tap your own to take it back.

features.title: Reply and quote
features.summary: Swipe right on a message — or long-press and choose Reply — to quote it above your answer; tap the quote to jump straight back to the original.

features.title: See when someone's typing
features.summary: A gentle “Maya is typing…” appears right in the chat and in the chat list, so you know a reply is on its way. In a group it rolls up to “3 people are typing…”.

features.title: Fix a typo — edit a message
features.summary: Long-press your own message and choose Edit within fifteen minutes of sending; the message keeps a small “edited” label so nothing ever feels hidden.

features.title: Delete a message
features.summary: “Delete for everyone” takes your message back for the whole family within two days — and a household admin can remove any message — while “Delete for me” simply tidies it away on your own device.

features.title: Animated stickers
features.summary: A sticker button opens a tray of big, playful animated stickers. The first pack is Nesta's own family-friendly set, built right in — no third-party service in the loop.

parts.title: Parents hold the keys
parts.dek: Oversight is about devices and membership — never message content.

features.title: Members roster
features.summary: Active members only, no “kid” labels anywhere. Admins get per-member actions; everyone sees the family.

features.title: Set up a device for anyone
features.summary: Admins mint a short-lived, single-use device grant for any member — the same QR + link + code hand-off as add-child.
features.entry: Members → member ⋮ → Set up a device

features.title: Add your own devices
features.summary: Any adult — admin or not — self-issues a grant for their tablet, browser, or next phone. Children always go through an adult (that's the security model, not a limitation).

features.title: Approve & revoke devices
features.summary: Every device is listed with its status; new web logins for kids wait for approval, and anything can be revoked instantly. The pending device waits on a friendly screen.

features.title: Your name is yours
features.summary: Everyone — kids included — edits how their own name appears. Renames re-label chats and the roster; admission and login are untouched.

features.title: Reset a child's password
features.summary: Only for the rarely-needed credentials fallback — a new one-time password, shown exactly once.

features.title: Remove a member
features.summary: Removes them entirely: devices stop being served, a child's hidden account is deleted, their name is freed — and past messages keep rendering under “old ‹name›”.

features.title: Sign out
features.summary: Confirms, revokes the session, and wipes this device's binding and local message mirror. Rejoining needs a fresh grant.
